data.title: Stock index futures higher as Broadcom issues upbeat outlook, eyes on jobs report
data.description:
Stock index futures climbed on Friday as Broadcom (AVGO) results brought cheer to the traders, while investors looked ahead to the key labor data.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.2%, and Dow futures (INDU) were unchanged.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was unchanged at 4.27%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.97%.

U.S. stocks were slammed lower Thursday, with the S&P 500 marking its lowest close since September as pressure from tech stocks, labor market weakness, and tariff-policy uncertainty took their toll on large-cap equities.

“Fierce cold winds were again blowing in U.S. equity markets yesterday, with ongoing trade policy volatility sending the S&P 500 to its worst day of 2025 for a second time this week,” Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said.

"Having imposed aggressive taxes on U.S. consumers at the start of the week, US President Trump partially retreated from taxing Mexican and Canadian imports yesterday (assuming goods are compliant with the revised NAFTA). The taxes are delayed until 2 April, but markets will likely price in more retreats," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Broadcom (AVGO) shares rose 12.7% in premarket trade after the semiconductor and software giant reported first-quarter results and guidance that topped expectations and suggested artificial intelligence spending has not slowed down.

Traders will keep on the key jobs data that will land before the opening bell today. The nonfarm payrolls report is expected to show that 160K jobs were added to the U.S. economy in February, according to the consensus of economists, up modestly from 143K jobs created in January. 

Looking forward to today, the main event is the U.S. jobs report, which should get even greater attention given the recent softening in U.S. data. Our U.S. economists expect payrolls to gain +160K in February, up from 143K in January, Sidorov added.

"Today’s U.S. employment report will not really reflect government job losses because the government is not a significant employer and because timing will push the losses into later months. The main future employment hit is likely to be from private sector businesses that depend (directly or indirectly) on government employment," Donovan added. 

Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell is also set to speak later in the day today.

data.title: Blackstone said to raise $8B for commercial CRE debt fund
data.description:
Blackstone (NYSE:BX) closed on an $8B commercial real estate debt fund this week, matching a record for the type of vehicle and providing some optimism for a rebound in the sector, according to a media report.

It took about two years for the world's largest private equity firm to raise the fund, which will invest in North America, Europe, and Australia, the Wall Street Journal reported on Friday, citing comments from a Blackstone (NYSE:BX) executive. The company's previous CRE debt fund, which also raised $8B, closed in September 2020.

The new fund, which started investing in late 2023, makes property loans and buys existing loans. CRE debt funds arose out of the global financial crisis as banks grew cautious about taking on real estate risk.

The sector has hit hard times as interest rates surged in the wake of the pandemic. The effect is particularly pronounced because the CRE sector is often highly leveraged. As such, property values typically drop when debt costs climb. Global fundraising by private equity firms that invest in real estate was $10B in Q4 2024, a five-year low, the WSJ said, citing Preqin data.

It's showing some signs of recovery in 2025 as debt markets have improved. Commercial mortgage-backed securities issuance has risen almost threefold in 2024, compared with 2023, the article said. And increased sales activity provides a clearer picture on property values.

Blackstone (BX) didn't immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Blackstone (BX) stock edged down 0.2% in Friday premarket trading.

data.title: Bayer seeks OK for potential capital raise over Roundup legal troubles
data.description:
Bayer (OTCPK:BAYRY) (OTCPK:BAYZF) -7% in Frankfurt trading Friday after saying it will seek shareholder approval at its annual meeting next month for a potential equity offer of 35% of its current share capital.

The proceeds of such a capital raise would be used exclusively for strengthening the balance sheet during a resolution of U.S. litigation involving products that were part of Bayer's (OTCPK:BAYRY) (OTCPK:BAYZF) Monsanto takeover, and not to finance mergers and acquisitions, Supervisory Board Chairman Norbert Winkeljohann said.

Bayer (OTCPK:BAYRY) (OTCPK:BAYZF) has already spent ~$10B of the $16B it had set aside for U.S. lawsuits over its Roundup weedkiller, which plaintiffs allege caused their cancer, and has agreed to pay nearly $2B to settle cases related to toxic PCBs, with more trials still ahead on both fronts.

"This step may be more of an insurance policy than anything substantive near-term," Berenberg analyst Sebastian Bray told Bloomberg, noting important appellate decisions from U.S. courts on both legal matters may be coming soon, so "the company simply wants to be prepared."

Bayer (OTCPK:BAYRY) (OTCPK:BAYZF) has told U.S. lawmakers it could stop selling Roundup unless the company can strengthen legal protection against product liability litigation, Reuters reported.

"Without regulatory clarity [the company] will need to exit the business. Bayer have been clear with legislators and farmer groups on this," Jefferies analysts said, citing guidance that company leadership provided in a meeting.

data.title: Black Diamond Group declares CAD 0.035 dividend
data.description:

data.title: aTyr rises after safety update on lead program
data.description:
• aTyr Pharma (NASDAQ:ATYR) traded higher in the premarket on Friday after the company said that an expert panel tasked with evaluating safety data in its lead program, EFZO-FIT, recommended that the trial be continued without any modifications.
  • The decision was based on EFZO-FIT’s fourth, pre-planned interim safety analysis by the trial’s independent data and safety monitoring board, the San Diego, California-headquartered biotech added.
  • The 52-week Phase 3 study is designed to evaluate aTyr’s (NASDAQ:ATYR) lead candidate efzofitimod in pulmonary sarcoidosis, a condition characterized by lumps or nodules in the lungs.
  • “The positive outcome from this fourth DSMB review for EFZO-FIT™ further reinforces our confidence in the favorable safety profile that we have seen for efzofitimod to date,” CEO Sanjay Shukla noted. The company remains on track to report topline data from the study in Q3 2025.

data.title: Land's End initiates process to explore strategic alternatives
data.description:
• Lands' End (NASDAQ:LE) has announced that its Board of Directors has initiated a process to explore strategic alternatives, including a sale, merger or similar transaction involving the Co., to maximize shareholder value.
  • “While we remain confident in the Co.’s potential for future value creation, the Board also believes that the market is undervaluing this great Co. and its upside potential.” said Josephine Linden, Chair of the Board.
  • "As a result, in consultation with the Board’s legal and financial advisors, we have determined it is an appropriate time to explore strategic alternatives to maximize shareholder value." Linden continued.
  • No assurances can be given as to the outcome or timing of the Board’s process.

data.title: Gannett announces additional debt reduction
data.description:
• Gannett (NYSE:GCI) said on Friday that it has prepaid approximately $57.5M on its five-year first lien term loan facility.
  • As a result of this prepayment, the company has reduced its first lien debt from $850M to $792.5M during the first quarter of 2025.
  • The debt repayment was funded with the proceeds from the sale of the Austin-American Statesmen that was completed on February 28, 2025, as well as cash on hand.

data.title: DeFi Technologies acquires majority stake in Swiss AI and asset management firm Neuronomics AG
data.description:
• DeFi Technologies (OTCQB:DEFTF) said on Friday that it has increased its stake in Neuronomics AG, a Swiss asset management firm specializing in artificial intelligence and model-driven quantitative trading strategies, to 52.5%.
  • This acquisition follows DeFi Technologies' earlier subscription to a capital increase in Neuronomics, where the company acquired a 10% stake.
  • In connection with the acquisition, the company issued 186,304 common shares of the company, plus additional cash considerations, to the selling shareholders of Neuronomics.

data.title: Primaris Real Estate Investment Trust renews normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• Primaris Real Estate Investment Trust (PMZ.UN:CA) said it has received approval from the Toronto Stock Exchange to renew its normal course issuer bid.
  • Under the bid, the firm may purchase for cancellation up to a maximum of 7.57M of its Series A units.
  • The bid will start on March 11 and remain in effect until the earlier of March 10, 2026.
